Review the parked content for completeness and correctness.
Post the parked document if you are authorized and business approval exists.
If the document should remain parked, communicate the pending action to the responsible user.
Verify afterward whether the document is now posted in accounting.

title: Purchase Order Item Deleted, Closed, or Delivery Completed
description: A procurement or invoice process fails because the relevant PO item is marked as deleted, fully delivered, or otherwise no longer open for follow-on processing.
steps: Open the purchase order and identify the affected item.
Check deletion indicator, final delivery indicator, and item completion status.
Review whether the business process is trying to use an item that is already finished.
Confirm whether the PO item should really be reopened or whether a new document is needed.
Adjust the item status only through the approved procurement process.
Retest the downstream step afterwards.

title: PO Confirmation Control Mismatch
description: A purchase order confirmation process fails because the item confirmation control setup does not match the confirmation category or business process being used.
steps: Identify the PO item and the expected confirmation behavior.
Check the confirmation control key on the item.
Review whether the received or entered confirmation matches the configured confirmation logic.
Correct the PO item setup or the confirmation process as appropriate.
Save the corrected PO data.
Recheck the item confirmation status afterwards.

title: Source List Entry Blocked
description: Automatic sourcing fails because a source list entry exists but is blocked or otherwise not selectable for procurement.
steps: Identify the material, plant, and supplier involved in sourcing.
Review the source list entries and their validity dates.
Check whether the desired source is marked as blocked or not fixed when expected.
Correct the source list only through the approved procurement master-data process.
Save the updated source-list entry.
Retest source determination afterwards.

title: Quota Arrangement Missing or Not Used
description: Procurement sourcing behaves unexpectedly because quota arrangement data is missing, expired, or not active for the material and plant.
steps: Identify the material, plant, and expected supplier selection logic.
Check whether a quota arrangement exists and is valid for the current date.
Review quota items, quota ratings, and source assignments.
Confirm whether source determination is expected to use quota arrangement in this scenario.
Maintain or correct the quota arrangement as needed.
Retest procurement source determination afterwards.

title: Requested Delivery Date Not Feasible
description: Sales or procurement scheduling behaves unexpectedly because the requested delivery date is in the past or not feasible based on lead times and availability.
steps: Identify the requested date and the scheduling context of the document.
Review confirmed dates, lead times, transit times, and ATP impact.
Check whether the requested date is historically invalid or operationally impossible.
Adjust the requested date or planning data according to the business need.
Redetermine dates if the transaction allows it.
Validate the new confirmed schedule afterwards.

title: Unexpected Delivery Split
description: A sales order creates multiple deliveries unexpectedly because header or item data differs in a way that prevents combined delivery creation.
steps: Identify the sales order items that split into separate deliveries.
Compare shipping-relevant fields such as shipping point, route, ship-to, delivery date, and plant.
Review whether the split is actually correct based on differing logistics data.
If not intended, align the relevant delivery-splitting criteria where business rules allow it.
Regenerate or review deliveries after the data is corrected.
Confirm whether the items now combine as expected.

title: Credit Memo or Return Requires Valid Reference
description: A credit or returns-related process fails because SAP expects a valid reference document that is missing or not suitable for the scenario.
steps: Identify the type of credit or returns document being created.
Check whether the process requires reference to an original sales, delivery, or billing document.
Validate that the reference document exists and is the correct one for the scenario.
Use the correct reference document when recreating or changing the transaction.
Review pricing and quantity carryover after adding the reference.
Save and verify the correction document afterwards.

title: Customer Return Cannot Be Processed
description: A returns process cannot continue because the return delivery, movement type, or stock handling setup is not aligned with the returns scenario.
steps: Identify the return order or delivery involved.
Review whether the return logistics process is correctly configured for the document type and item category.
Check stock destination, movement type, and serial or batch constraints if relevant.
Correct the process data or use the appropriate returns step according to business rules.
Save and continue the returns document processing.
Verify the stock and status outcome afterwards.

title: Warehouse Quant or Bin Stock Mismatch
description: Warehouse stock appears inconsistent because quant-level stock, transfer activity, or inventory results do not align with expectations.
steps: Identify the warehouse number, bin, and material involved.
Review the current quant situation and compare it with the expected warehouse status.
Check recent transfer orders, confirmations, or inventory differences affecting the bin.
Determine whether the mismatch is operational, timing-related, or master-data related.
Correct the warehouse situation through the proper WM process.
Recheck bin and quant status afterwards.

title: Warehouse Transfer Order Still Open
description: A warehouse movement remains incomplete because the transfer order was created but not confirmed.
steps: Identify the transfer order and the warehouse process it belongs to.
Check whether the transfer order is still open or only partially confirmed.
Review the physical warehouse status and confirm whether execution really happened.
If appropriate, confirm the transfer order using the correct warehouse process.
Validate the resulting stock and process status afterwards.
Recheck any linked delivery or warehouse requirement if relevant.

title: Planned Order Not Firmed
description: MRP changes or reschedules a planned order unexpectedly because the proposal was not firmed or protected from replanning.
steps: Identify the planned order that changed unexpectedly.
Review whether the proposal is firmed or still fully changeable by MRP.
Check planning dates and why the next MRP run adjusted the proposal.
Firm the planned order if business rules require it to stay stable.
Save the planning changes and review the result.
Monitor the next planning cycle to confirm the behavior is now correct.

title: Backflush Did Not Post Component Consumption
description: Production confirmation completes, but expected automatic component consumption does not post.
steps: Identify the production order, operation, and components involved.
Check whether the order and BOM components are set up for backflush behavior.
Review the confirmation result to see whether only yield posted without goods movement.
Check stock, movement type, and component assignment context.
Correct the master or order setup if backflush should have happened automatically.
Retest the confirmation or post the required movement through the approved process.

title: Order Status TECO or Closed Blocking Further Processing
description: A production or maintenance order cannot continue because it is technically completed, closed, or otherwise no longer open for the action being attempted.
steps: Identify the order number and the blocked action.
Review the current system status and whether TECO, CLSD, or another closing status is set.
Confirm whether the order should really be reopened or whether the action belongs on another order.
Adjust the order status only through the approved business process.
Save the status change if one is made.
Retest the original action afterwards.

title: HR Infotype Record Locked
description: An HR master-data change cannot be made because the employee or infotype record is locked by another user or process.
steps: Identify the employee number and infotype being maintained.
Confirm that another HR user or process is not actively editing the same record.
Review lock entries if the record appears stuck or unavailable.
Ask the owning user to complete or exit the transaction if possible.
Clear stale locks only when you are sure no active work will be affected.
Retry the HR maintenance afterwards.

pattern: org change not visible in workflow
title: Manager or Workflow Structure Not Updated Yet
description: A manager, approver, or self-service process does not reflect the latest employee or organizational changes because structural data is not yet aligned or refreshed.
steps: Identify the employee, manager, and missing relationship in the business process.
Review the OM structure and holder relationships for the relevant position.
Confirm whether the HR master and OM structures are aligned.
Correct any missing structural assignment using the proper OM or HR process.
Allow the process context to refresh and verify the manager relationship again.
Retest the workflow or self-service scenario afterwards.

title: Spool or TemSe Storage Issue
description: Output generation or background reporting fails because spool or TemSe-related storage or administration issues prevent new spool requests from being created correctly.
steps: Identify whether the issue occurs during report execution or output creation.
Check recent spool and system-log behavior around the failure.
Review whether spool administration or TemSe housekeeping is needed.
Coordinate cleanup or technical correction through the proper basis process.
Retry the report or output generation afterwards.
Confirm that spool requests can now be created normally.

title: Background Job Scheduled but Not Starting
description: A background job remains scheduled or released but does not actually start at the expected time.
steps: Check the job status, start condition, and planned execution time.
Review whether the execution window or server assignment is valid.
Confirm there is no dependency or event condition preventing start.
Check whether background work processes are available and healthy.
Correct the scheduling setup or technical bottleneck as appropriate.
Monitor the job again to confirm it starts normally.

title: HTTP Destination Authentication Failed
description: An outbound HTTP or REST call fails because the configured destination authentication is missing, expired, or invalid.
steps: Identify the HTTP destination and the interface call that failed.
Test the destination and review the exact authentication error.
Check credentials, tokens, certificates, or trusted-authentication setup depending on the scenario.
Confirm whether the target service still expects the same authentication method.
Correct the destination authentication in the approved technical process.
Retest the outbound call afterwards.

title: Search Help or Input Help Returns No Values
description: A field's input help opens but shows no values because selection context, authorizations, or underlying search-help data does not return usable entries.
steps: Identify the exact field and transaction where the empty input help occurs.
Check whether the search help depends on organizational context or prerequisite field values.
Review whether the underlying customizing or master data actually contains values.
Consider whether the user might be restricted from seeing the relevant values.
Correct the missing context or data source according to the application design.
Retest the input help afterwards.
